rationale: Khan and Dhar (2006) is cited in the core framing of the paper as prior evidence that reasons/justifications shape indulgence choices. The citing authors use this prior result to motivate their gap: earlier work focused on choice, preferences, or behavior, not on consumers' actual affective experience while indulging. The citation therefore helps position the contribution rather than serving as a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Khan and Dhar (2006) as part of the empirical foundation that justifications matter in indulgence decisions, then adds a new layer by testing whether those justifications also change experienced feelings during consumption. In this sense, it uses the cited work to set up and motivate its own contribution.
